description: kernel_step_1 and kernel_step_2 sub-kernel family in genesis/engine/solvers/rigid/rigid_solver.py. Includes mass_mat_assemble (kernel_5), factor_mass (kernel_10, already DSL-retuned by (redacted) - reference pattern), kernel_11, kernel_25, and update_cartesian_space (step_2_kernel_9). Multiple sub-kernels visible in rocprofv3 as kernel_step_1_c498_*_kernel_N_* and kernel_step_2_c500_*_kernel_N_*.

edit_files.rationale: kernel_step_1 defined at line ~2559 (@qd.kernel(fastcache=...)) and kernel_step_2 at line ~2622. Both are large multi-stage kernels that decompose into many numbered subkernels in the rocprofv3 stats.

Contains func_factor_mass at line 574 -- the proven DSL-retune pattern ((redacted), TICKET, 71% kernel reduction via BLOCK_DIM=64 + wave64 comment). Read first as the canonical reference for what a winning DSL change in this family looks like.

correctness_pytest_extra_args: -k "step or rigid_solver or factor_mass or cartesian or mass_mat_assemble or not (test_convexify or test_mesh_repair or test_mesh_primitive_COM)"
